contentBlocks.original:
शिवो महेश्वर-श्शम्भुः पिनाकी शशिशेखरः
वामदेवो विरूपाक्षः कपर्दी नीललोहितः ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivō mahēśvara-śśambhuḥ pinākī śaśiśēkharaḥ
vāmadēvō virūpākṣaḥ kapardī nīlalōhitaḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shiva (the auspicious), Maheshvara (great lord), Shambhu (bestower of joy), Pinaki (bearer of the Pinaka bow), Shashishekhara (crescent moon-crested), Vamadeva (the gracious deity), Virupaksha (three-eyed one), Kapardi (bearer of matted locks), Nilalohita (blue-throated and red-bodied).

contentBlocks.original:
शङ्कर-श्शूलपाणिश्च खट्वाङ्गी विष्णुवल्लभः
शिपिविष्टोऽम्बिकानाथः श्रीकण्ठो भक्तवत्सलः ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaṅkara-śśūlapāṇiścha khaṭvāṅgī viṣṇuvallabhaḥ
śipiviṣṭō'mbikānāthaḥ śrīkaṇṭhō bhaktavatsalaḥ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shankara (giver of peace), Shulapani (trident-bearer), Khatvangi (bearer of the skull-staff), Vishnuvallabha (dear to Vishnu), Shipivishta (pervader of all light), Ambikanatha (lord of Ambika/Parvati), Shrikantha (of auspicious throat), Bhaktavatsala (affectionate to devotees).

contentBlocks.original:
भव-श्शर्व-स्त्रिलोकेशः शितिकण्ठः शिवाप्रियः
उग्रः कपाली कामारि रन्धकासुरसूदनः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhava-śśarva-strilōkēśaḥ śitikaṇṭhaḥ śivāpriyaḥ
ugraḥ kapālī kāmāri randhakāsurasūdanaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bhava (pure existence itself), Sharva (the archer/destroyer), Trilokisha (lord of the three worlds), Shitikantha (blue-throated), Shivapriya (beloved of Parvati), Ugra (the fierce one), Kapali (skull-bearer), Kamari (enemy of Kama), Andhakasura Sudana (destroyer of the demon Andhaka).

contentBlocks.original:
गङ्गाधरो ललाटाक्षः कालकालः कृपानिधिः
भीमः परशुहस्तश्च मृगपाणि-र्जटाधरः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gaṅgādharō lalāṭākṣaḥ kālakālaḥ kṛpānidhiḥ
bhīmaḥ paraśuhastaścha mṛgapāṇi-rjaṭādharaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Gangadhara (bearer of the Ganga), Lalataksha (forehead-eyed), Kalakala (the death of death), Kripanithi (treasure-house of compassion), Bhima (the awesome), Parashuhasta (axe-bearing), Mrigapani (bearing a deer in hand), Jatandhara (bearer of matted locks).

contentBlocks.original:
कैलासवासी कवची कठोर-स्त्रिपुरान्तकः
वृषाङ्को वृषभारूढो भस्मोद्धूलितविग्रहः ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kailāsavāsī kavachī kaṭhōra-stripurāntakaḥ
vṛṣāṅkō vṛṣabhārūḍhō bhasmōddhūḻitavigrahaḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kailasavasi (dweller of Kailash), Kavachi (the armored one), Kathora (the firm and hard), Tripurantaka (destroyer of the three cities), Vrishanka (bull-banner bearer), Vrishabharudha (mounted on the bull), Bhasmoddhulitatvigraha (body radiant with sacred ash).

Samapriya (lover of Sama Veda chanting), Svaramaya (made of musical notes), Trayimurthi (embodiment of the three Vedas), Anishvara (lord without a superior), Sarvajnya (omniscient), Paramatma (the Supreme Self), Somasurya-agnilocana (whose eyes are moon, sun and fire).

contentBlocks.original:
हवि-र्यज्ञमय-स्सोमः पञ्चवक्त्र-स्सदाशिवः
विश्वेश्वरो वीरभद्रो गणनाथः प्रजापतिः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
havi-ryajñamaya-ssōmaḥ pañchavaktra-ssadāśivaḥ
viśvēśvarō vīrabhadrō gaṇanāthaḥ prajāpatiḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Havi (the oblation itself), Yajnyamaya (pervading all sacrifice), Soma (the moon-nectar), Panchavaktra (five-faced), Sadashiva (eternally auspicious), Vishveshvara (lord of the universe), Virabhadra (the heroic and auspicious), Gananatha (lord of the ganas), Prajapati (lord of all creatures).

contentBlocks.original:
हिरण्यरेता दुर्धर्षो गिरीशो गिरिशोऽनघः
भुजङ्गभूषणो भर्गो गिरिधन्वा गिरिप्रियः ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hiraṇyarētā durdharṣō girīśō giriśō'naghaḥ
bhujaṅgabhūṣaṇō bhargō giridhanvā giripriyaḥ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hiranyareta (of golden seed), Durdarsha (difficult to behold), Girisha (lord of the mountain), Girisha (who plays on the mountain — a distinct epithet), Anagha (the sinless one), Bhujangabhushana (adorned with serpents), Bharga (radiant effulgence), Giridhanva (mountain-bowed), Giripriya (beloved of the mountain/Parvati).

Krittivasas (clothed in animal hide), Purarati (enemy of Tripura), Bhagavan (the blessed Lord), Pramathaadhipa (chief of the Pramatha hosts), Mrityunjaya (conqueror of death), Sukshmatanu (of subtle body), Jagadvyapi (pervading the universe), Jagadguru (teacher of the world).

contentBlocks.original:
व्योमकेशो महासेनजनक-श्चारुविक्रमः
रुद्रो भूतपतिः स्थाणु-रहिर्भुध्न्यो दिगम्बरः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vyōmakēśō mahāsēnajanaka-śchāruvikramaḥ
rudrō bhūtapatiḥ sthāṇu-rahirbhudhnyō digambaraḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vyomakesha (sky-haired), Mahasenajanaka (father of Kartikeya), Charuvikrama (of beautiful valor), Rudra (the fierce howling one), Bhutapati (lord of all beings), Sthanu (the immovable pillar), Ahirbudhnya (the cosmic serpent of the deep), Digambara (sky-clad).

Ashtamurthi (of eight forms), Aneekatma (of innumerable selves), Sattvika (of pure sattva nature), Shuddhavigraha (of pure form), Shashvata (the eternal), Khandaparashur (bearing the broken axe), Aja (the unborn), Pashavimochaka (liberator from all bondage).

contentBlocks.original:
मृडः पशुपति-र्देवो महादेवोऽव्ययो हरिः
पूषदन्तभि-दव्यग्रो दक्षाध्वरहरो हरः ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mṛḍaḥ paśupati-rdēvō mahādēvō'vyayō hariḥ
pūṣadantabhi-davyagrō dakṣādhvaraharō haraḥ ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Mrida (the kind and gentle), Pashupati (lord of all souls), Deva (the divine one), Mahadeva (the great god), Avyaya (the imperishable), Hari (the remover of sins), Pushadadantabhida (who broke Pusha's teeth at Daksha's sacrifice), Avyagra (the undisturbed), Dakshadhvarahara (destroyer of Daksha's sacrifice), Hara (the remover).

Bhaganetrabhida (who destroyed Bhaga's eyes at the sacrifice), Avyakta (the unmanifest), Sahasraksha (thousand-eyed), Sahasrapat (thousand-footed), Apavargaprada (grantor of final liberation), Ananta (the infinite), Taraka (the liberating guide), Parameshvara (the supreme Lord of all).
